commit.message:
[docs] docs: reduce bloat in distributions/vanilla.mdx (#3256)

* docs: reduce bloat in distributions/vanilla.mdx

Condense Quick Start from 4 step-by-step sections to a single code
block (removing duplicate generic workflow already in use-cases.mdx),
trim Registry Mirrors example, simplify Advanced Topics (Multi-Node,
Kubernetes Version Selection, Custom CNI), and condense troubleshooting
items 3 and 4 with cross-reference links.

21% reduction (247 → 195 lines). All essential technical content
preserved: Kind-specific config, LoadBalancer, troubleshooting,
advanced topics.

commit.message:
Fix boot progress: only intercept assembly/dotnetwasm, return null for dotnetjs

Blazor requires dotnetjs resources to return a URI string or null,
not a Promise<Response>. Now only intercepts 'assembly' and 'dotnetwasm'
resource types for progress tracking; all other types fall through to
default Blazor loading.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Show progress bar during WASM boot with per-assembly status

Replace the static 'Loading...' text with a progress bar that shows
each assembly/resource being downloaded during Blazor WASM startup.
Uses Blazor's loadBootResource callback to intercept fetches and
update a progress bar, counter, and current resource name in real time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix sample dropdown: guard against DetachFromSample during programmatic loads, format XML docs in tooltips

Two fixes:
1. Sample selection immediately reset to 'Custom project' because setting
   editor content fired OnSchemaContentChanged -> DetachFromSample. Added
   isLoadingContent guard flag set during sample loads, new project, open
   project, and auto-save restore.
2. 'New' button showed 'Data Object' because activeSampleId was set to a
   non-existent option value. Now uses null consistently for non-sample
   states, matching the '-- Custom project --' placeholder.
3. Signature help tooltips showed raw XML documentation. Added XML doc
   parser that extracts summary, returns, remarks, and per-parameter docs
   into clean Markdown text. JS side passes docs as MarkdownString for
   proper rendering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Task.Yield() before heavy sync work to improve UI responsiveness

In single-threaded Blazor WASM, synchronous Roslyn compilation and code
generation block the UI thread entirely. Add Task.Yield() calls before
heavy work in GenerateAsync, RunAsync, live diagnostics, and
CompilationService.CompileAsync so the browser can render status updates
(spinner, progress text) before the thread is blocked.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: Cattle Health Monitoring System

- C_RESTAPI: FastAPI backend with MongoDB Atlas
  - JWT + API key authentication
  - Cattle CRUD, sensor data ingestion (bulk)
  - Health evaluation with threshold-based alerts
  - Email notifications with 48h health graphs
  - Role-based access (super_admin/admin/user)

- C_FRONTEND: Streamlit dashboard
  - MongoDB-inspired dark/light theme (default: light)
  - Role-based dashboards (Super Admin/Admin/User)
  - Multi-language support (English/Tamil/Hindi)
  - Real-time cattle health graphs (Plotly)
  - Cattle management, user management, alerts
  - Integrated logo and professional UI

- .gitignore configured (venv, .env, __pycache__, IDE files)
- .env.example files for both frontend and backend

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: configurable logging levels and file-based log output (#26)

Adds --log-level (off/info/debug) and --log-file flags for configurable,
file-based logging. Default log path: ~/.kubectl-mcp/server-<pid>.log.

- LogLevel type and parser in internal/mcplog
- HTTP logging transport for K8s API calls at debug level
- Level-aware MCP server hooks (off=no-ops, info=summaries, debug=full)
- Transport wrapper support in ClientPool
- PID-based log filenames to avoid concurrent instance conflicts
- E2E tests for all log levels
- Full TDD with unit tests for every component

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Migrate data fetching to Alpha Vantage MCP + async Copilot SDK agentic workflow (#30)

* feat: migrate data fetching from yfinance to Alpha Vantage MCP server

commit.message:
perf(notify): cache color objects and precompute indent strings (#3252)

Replace per-call fcolor.New() allocations in getMessageConfig with
package-level cached *fcolor.Color variables. Also precompute the
multiline indent string as a messageConfig field (symbolIndent const)
instead of recomputing strings.Repeat + rune-width on every call.

Benchmarks (AMD EPYC 7763, linux/amd64):

  BenchmarkWriteMessage_SingleLine  255→143 ns/op  80→32 B/op   5→2 allocs/op  (−44%/−60%/−60%)
  BenchmarkWriteMessage_WithArgs    514→361 ns/op  160→112 B/op 7→4 allocs/op  (−30%/−30%/−43%)
  BenchmarkWriteMessage_Multiline   562→402 ns/op  256→208 B/op 10→7 allocs/op (−28%/−19%/−30%)
  BenchmarkWriteMessage_WithTimer   510→419 ns/op  112→68 B/op  9→6 allocs/op  (−18%/−39%/−33%)
  BenchmarkWriteMessage_AllTypes   1904→1080 ns/op 560→224 B/op 35→14 allocs/op(−43%/−60%/−60%)

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>

commit.message:
Crayotter launch update: CO header icon

- Replace website header logo image with gradient text mark 'CO'\n- Keep brand text as Crayotter\n- Preserve all other website behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Monaco race condition: delay Blazor start until Monaco loaded

BlazorMonaco silently fails (console error, no retry) if the monaco
global isn't available when it tries to create editors. On hard refresh,
the AMD require for vs/editor/editor.main may not complete before
Blazor renders the StandaloneCodeEditor components.

Fix: use autostart=false on blazor.webassembly.js and gate Blazor.start()
on a monacoReady promise that resolves when the AMD require completes.
The require() call is issued before jsInterop.js loads (which clobbers
the AMD require with a plain config object).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: move cipher decrypt to cipher_write MCP tool (#3245)

* Initial plan

* feat: move cipher decrypt to cipher_write MCP tool

Mark `cipher decrypt` as a write operation (`ai.toolgen.permission: write`)
since it reveals secrets and can write decrypted output to disk via --output.

This consolidates all cipher subcommands (encrypt, decrypt, edit, import)
into a single `cipher_write` tool, reducing the total from 6 to 5 MCP tools.

Add TestWriteToolSubcommandCoverage to validate that cluster_write contains
update, workload_write contains apply/reconcile, and cipher_write contains
encrypt/decrypt.

Update docs (mcp.mdx, architecture.mdx, copilot-instructions.md) to reflect
the 5-tool layout.

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devantler-tech/ksail/sessions/6227dab4-4e93-4174-b7a7-060ad02766a0

* fix: resolve golangci-lint issues in TestWriteToolSubcommandCoverage

Refactor into subtests with a helper to fix cyclop complexity (12 > 10)
and varnamelen ('ok' too short). Fix golines formatting on long error string.

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devantler-tech/ksail/sessions/1f9296e3-a135-40b3-b99f-27fcc618bab5

Bumps [github.com/anchore/syft](https://github.com/anchore/syft) from 1.38.2 to 1.42.3.
- [Release notes](https://github.com/anchore/syft/releases)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/anchore/syft/blob/main/RELEASE.md)
- [Commits](https://github.com/anchore/syft/compare/v1.38.2...v1.42.3)

---
updated-dependencies:
- dependency-name: github.com/anchore/syft
  dependency-version: 1.42.3
  dependency-type: indirect
...

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>

* fix(deps): remove runtime-spec v1.2.1 pin to fix containerd/v2 type incompatibility (#3248)

* Initial plan

* fix(deps): remove runtime-spec v1.2.1 pin to fix containerd/v2 type incompatibility

The replace directive pinning runtime-spec to v1.2.1 was needed when
containerd v1.7.x's oci package was compiled (LinuxPids.Limit changed
from int64 to *int64 in v1.3.0). However, `go mod why` confirms that
containerd v1.7.30's oci package is NOT imported by the build graph.

With syft v1.42.3 introducing containerd/v2 v2.2.1 (which IS compiled
and requires runtime-spec v1.3.0), the pin must be removed to allow
both containerd versions to coexist.

Verified: `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` pass with this change.

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devantler-tech/ksail/sessions/479d8f02-3258-421e-bd4b-b3f1bdc94135

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(test): use html content check for builds page state test

isVisible() can return false for elements that are in the DOM but
not yet layout-painted in CI's headless Chromium (e.g. flex containers
with CSS variables not yet resolved). Use page.content() string check
instead, which verifies static HTML presence independent of layout.

commit.message:
fix(builds): cap cold-start API calls + 8min step timeout

- MaxRunsPerWf: 30 — limits new runs fetched per workflow file,
  bounding cold-start at ~510 job API calls max (17 wf × 30 runs)
- 8min timeout on fetch-builds CI step (continue-on-error already set)
- Warm incremental runs still fetch all new runs (cap only applies
  when record count per-workflow == MaxRunsPerWf)

commit.message:
fix(builds): reduce cold-start lookback to 14 days

90-day cold-start caused ~21min fetch-builds runs due to job-level API
calls across 17 workflow files. Warm incremental runs already use
latest-1d automatically; cold-start now bootstraps with 14 days which
completes in ~2-3 minutes.

commit.message:
feat: add Bluefin CI/CD Build Pipeline dashboard tab

Add a new '🏗️ Builds' tab tracking GitHub Actions pipelines across
7 repos: ublue-os/bluefin, ublue-os/bluefin-lts, and 5 projectbluefin
repos (common, dakota, iso, finpilot, testhub).

## Go backend (stats-go/internal/builds/)
- types.go: WorkflowRunRecord, JobRecord, RepoConfig, all output types
- metrics.go: SuccessRate, Percentile, MTTR, MTBF, FlakinessIndex,
  ClassifyFailure, ParseJobDimensions, ComputeDORALevel, ComputeSummary,
  ComputeRepoMetrics, ComputeTopFlaky, ComputeDurationTrend,
  ComputeFailureBreakdown, ComputeTriggerBreakdown, computeDORA
- metrics_test.go: 44 table-driven tests covering all metric functions
- collector.go: paginated GitHub Actions API collector with dedup,
  6-month history pruning, writes src/data/builds.json +
  .sync-cache/builds-history.json
- main.go: fetch-builds subcommand wired in

## Frontend (src/)
- src/data/builds.json: seed data (bootstrap state)
- src/lib/types.ts: 12 new interfaces (BuildsData, PipelineSummary,
  DORAMetrics, FlakyJob, RecentBuild, DurationBucket, FailureCategory,
  TriggerBreakdown, BuildDailySnapshot, BuildRepoMetrics,
  BuildStreamMetrics, BuildArchMetrics)
- src/components/TabNav.astro: added 'builds' tab
- src/layouts/Layout.astro: extended activeTab union type
- src/pages/builds/index.astro: full page shell with all 11 components

## Chart components (src/components/builds/)
- BuildsHealthBar.astro: 5-card KPI strip (status, rate, builds, duration, DORA)
- DORAPanel.astro: 2x2 DORA metrics grid with benchmark thresholds
- BuildSuccessChart.astro: success rate line + daily outcomes stacked bar
- BuildDurationChart.astro: P50/P95/P99 trend + per-repo horizontal bar
- FailureAnalysisChart.astro: failure category doughnut + MTTR/MTBF KPIs
- FlakinessLeaderboard.astro: top flaky jobs table with colored index bars
- PipelineThroughputChart.astro: builds/day + trigger doughnut + queue time
- BuilderComparison.astro: cross-repo comparison table + stream health matrix
- MatrixCoverageChart.astro: expected vs active streams grouped bar
- PublishReliabilityChart.astro: publish reliability KPI strip
- RecentBuildsFeed.astro: recent builds table with relative timestamps

## Testing
- src/lib/schema.test.ts: 8 new schema tests for builds.json (110 total pass)
- tests/e2e/builds.spec.ts: 8 defensive E2E tests (pass in bootstrap state)

## CI/CD
- .github/workflows/daily-build.yml: fetch-builds step + builds data verify
- Justfile: sync-builds recipe + included in sync target

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add cmd/streak binary for cross-machine session streak tracking (#181)

Computes current and longest session streaks from exported-sessions.json
manifest (committed to git, covers all machines). Earnable per-machine —
run `just streak` to check. Fires circuit_unbroken triumph at 30 days.

- cmd/streak/main.go: reads SessionTimestamps from manifest, computes
  current_streak (active today/yesterday grace), longest_streak, total_active_days,
  last_session_date; writes data/streak.json; appends triumph_earned event
  for circuit_unbroken when current_streak >= 30 (deduplicates via event log scan)
- cmd/streak/main_test.go: unit tests for streak computation, consecutiveDays,
  parseDate covering edge cases (empty, single, gap, broken, cross-month)
- Justfile: adds `streak` target (`go run ./cmd/streak/`)
- exporter: rebuilt binary to match already-committed source

commit.message:
fix: add missing triumph_earned events to back earned state in triumphs.json

Four triumphs (first_session, first_knowledge, new_skill, site_launched)
had earned:true in triumphs.json but zero backing events in the events log.
Per EVENTS.md, GHA regenerates earned state from scratch on every compute run
— all four would have been silently wiped on the next workflow trigger.

Appended events using timestamps already recorded in triumphs.json.
compute.py confirms: 4 earned IDs found, no state drift.

Fixes: di1 (critical) from 4-agent code review 2026-03-22

- Non-maleficence requirements (§2.8.4)
- Code of Ethics in practice example (§2.9.3)
- MORAL model case application (§2.10.4)
- Individual moral resilience strategies (§2.11.4)
- Institutional moral resilience strategies (§2.11.4)
- Ethics committee membership (§2.12.1)
- Ethics consultation triggers (§2.12.2)
- SDOH clinical examples (§2.13.2)
- Racialized health disparity examples (§2.13.3)

All clinical facts preserved. Tables, blockquotes, sidebars, learning
objectives, key terms, and NCLEX question answer choices unchanged.
Bullet count reduced from ~109 to 0 standalone bullets.

commit.message:
Enhance type map: enum/const values, collapsible nodes, search filter

- Show enum values as nested list under enum types (supports heterogeneous)
- Show const values inline on type entries and composition members
- Recurse into composition member types so oneOf/anyOf/allOf members
  appear as their own generated types in the tree
- InferKind detects 'enum' and 'const' kinds
- Collapsible tree nodes with expand/collapse chevrons
- Auto-expand on type selection; force-expand when filtering
- Search/filter box to find types by name, kind, property, or value

commit.message:
Add dark/light mode theming and console clear button

- Extract all ~60 hardcoded colors in app.css to CSS custom properties
- Add complete light theme via [data-theme='light'] and prefers-color-scheme media query
- Add theme toggle button in toolbar (cycles Auto → Light → Dark)
- Persist theme preference in localStorage, auto-detect system preference
- Monaco editors switch between 'vs-dark' and 'vs' themes
- Anti-flash inline script in index.html applies theme before first paint
- System theme change listener updates playground when OS preference changes
- Add console clear button (🗑️) to console toolbar

commit.message:
fix: add workflows permission and stale discussion cleanup for agentic workflows (#3241)

* Initial plan

* fix: add workflows permission and improve phase selection for agentic workflows

- Add permission-workflows: write to daily-workflow-maintenance GitHub App
  token to allow pushing .lock.yml files to .github/workflows/
- Add deterministic pre-step for discussion detection in daily-builder,
  daily-code-quality, and daily-workflow-maintenance to prevent Phase 1
  re-runs when a planning discussion already exists
- Add close-older-discussions: true to all three workflows to prevent
  stale discussion accumulation
- Recompile all affected lock files with gh aw compile (v0.62.5)

Fix build.ps1: create destination dirs before copying source files

Copy-Item at Step 0 to fail.
